summary: Fetches HackerNews stories from the Firebase REST API with concurrent item fetching and checkpoint-based deduplication

requires: Reachable HackerNews Firebase API (or override via WithBaseURL)
Checkpoint is a decimal integer string (highest story ID seen)
guarantees: Returns stories in original feed order (insertion-sort after concurrent fetch)
Checkpoint is always the highest story ID seen across all fetched items, even filtered/deleted ones
Checkpoint is preserved across calls with no new stories
Never returns deleted, dead, or non-story items

effects: HTTP GET to hacker-news.firebaseio.com (or baseURL override)
Up to limit+1 HTTP requests per FetchMessages call (1 for IDs + up to limit for items)
state: Stateless between calls; baseURL field supports test injection via WithBaseURL

observations.text: Item fetching is concurrent with a semaphore of 10 goroutines. Items that fail to fetch are silently dropped (fetchErr != nil returns without adding). This means fewer results than expected may be returned without error.
Checkpoint tracks highest ID across ALL fetched items including deleted/dead/non-story, so subsequent calls won't re-fetch those items even if they were filtered. This is correct but subtle.

Uses http.DefaultClient with no timeout — context cancellation is the only protection against hung requests. A hung Firebase connection will block up to 10 goroutines indefinitely if the context is not bounded.
limitOverride in FetchMessages takes precedence over the struct's limit field when > 0. This allows per-call override of the configured default.
WithBaseURL builder method enables test injection without interfaces — consistent with the pattern used in RedditSource and implicitly in RSSSource (feedURL field).
URL for each story is always the HN permalink (news.ycombinator.com/item?id=N), not the story's external URL. The story's own URL appears in the Text field via composeText.
